seo.title: Live AI prototyping for 15–500 people · workshops, hackathons, keynotes
seo.description: Hands-on AI sessions on your real workflows. Teams leave with a working prototype, not slides. 90 minutes to 2 days, 15 to 500 people, in Romania and across the EU.
seo.keywords: AI keynote speaker, AI workshop, AI hackathon, live AI prototyping, corporate AI event, Copilot workshop, Gemini workshop, AI training event Bucharest, AI conference Romania, EU

intro.kicker: Why most AI events don't work
intro.titleStart: They show what AI can do.
intro.titleEm: Not what it does inside your company.
intro.intro: People watch a demo. Maybe try something once. Then go back to old workflows by Tuesday morning. The reason isn't the tool, it's that nothing in the session connected to their actual work. Their reports. Their client calls. Their weekly prep. When AI doesn't map to a specific task someone does every day, it stays a curiosity.
intro.pullQuoteStart: Before every session, I research the company, the sector, and the teams in the room.
intro.pullQuoteEm: Then we build, live, on a real problem one of the teams brought that morning.
intro.pullQuoteEnd: Everyone leaves with prompt sheets they can copy-paste on Monday morning.

inside.intro: A standard half-day workshop, minute by minute. The exact same shape scales up to a 2-day hackathon or down to a 90-minute keynote with a live build segment.

steps.title: Intake
steps.body: Each team brings one real problem they have this week. A stuck report, a slow client process, a research task no one wants to do. We pick the sharpest one to build on first.

steps.title: Live build, with the failures
steps.body: I prototype the first solution in front of the room, on that team's problem, inside the AI tools you already pay for. Wrong outputs included - that's where teams learn to actually trust the tool.

steps.title: Hands-on, every team builds
steps.body: Each team works on their own problem. I move between tables, unblock, push the prompts further, show shortcuts. Nobody watches. Everyone builds.

steps.title: Showcase + sharpening
steps.body: Each team demos what they built. The room critiques, I sharpen the prompts on the spot, and we capture the patterns that worked across teams.

steps.title: Walk out with
steps.body: A working prototype per team, a copy-paste prompt sheet built from what worked in the room, and a one-page plan for what to ship next week.

formats.titleEnd: real prototypes, not slides about prototypes.

items.title: Keynote + live build
items.body: Built for conferences and large corporate events. I close every keynote with a live AI build on a problem volunteered from the audience, so the room sees the workflow, not just the theory.
items.outcome: Walk out with: the recording, the prompt sheet, and one fully built example from your sector.

items.title: Hands-on workshop
items.body: The full live prototyping format above. Participants work inside the AI tools they already have access to, on the actual problems they brought into the room. Real failures included.
items.outcome: Walk out with: 1 working prototype per team + copy-paste prompt sheet + next-week plan.

items.title: Internal hackathon
items.body: Full design and facilitation of an internal AI hackathon. Brief design, team coaching during the build, judging criteria, and a final showcase to leadership. Teams ship working prototypes on real company problems.
items.outcome: Walk out with: 5–10 working prototypes, judged and ranked, plus a shortlist ready for adoption.

items: Live facilitation and live prototyping by Răzvan Vâlceanu, founder of The Unlearning School
Real prototypes built in the room on your problems, not slideware about prototypes
Pre-event research on the company, sector, and specific teams in the room
All content and demos built around your tool stack and industry context
Copy-paste prompt sheets every participant takes out of the room
Post-session resource pack: prompts, workflow examples, next steps
Optional: scoped proposal for follow-up adoption work based on what surfaces in the room

pricing.body: A 30-minute call is enough to confirm the format, the audience, and the right scope. From there we send a specific proposal within 48 hours.
pricing.italic: No catalogue pricing. Every event is built around the room it is delivered in.

items.q: How long do you need before the event?
items.a: Two to three weeks is ideal - that gives me time to research your company, your sector, and the teams in the room. Tighter timelines work too; we just narrow the prep scope.

items.q: What if our teams don't already use AI tools?
items.a: It's not a blocker. We work in whatever you have access to - Copilot, ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude - and if there's no access at all, I bring a working setup to demo on. Live prototyping still happens.

items.q: Do participants need to bring laptops or prep anything?
items.a: Yes - laptops, and one real problem each team has this week. That's the only prep. The sharper the problem, the sharper the prototype we build on it.

items.q: Can you deliver in English and Romanian?
items.a: Both. Sessions are delivered in the language of the room. Most prompt sheets and post-session materials ship bilingually by default.

items.q: Do you sign NDAs and handle confidential context?
items.a: Always. NDA signed before any pre-event research starts. Nothing built on real client problems leaves the room without your approval.
Every participant leaves with a copy-paste prompt sheet and a one-page next-week plan. Optional: a scoped proposal for follow-up adoption work, based on what surfaced in the room.

funding.body: Microsoft, AWS, and Google allocate co-sell and Market Development Funds every quarter to support exactly this kind of adoption work. Most companies qualify. Most don't know it. We structure the program so it does.

cta.title: Bring this to your team.
cta.subtitle: A 30-minute call to confirm format, audience, and scope. Specific proposal within 48 hours. Built around the people in the room, not the previous event we ran.
cta.note: Free · No commitment · Proposal within 48 hours
